title: Question Formation
description: Stop just talking and start asking! Learn how to ask W-questions and Yes/No questions.
explanation:
**HOOK**
Asking questions in German is like flipping a coin. For 'Yes/No' questions, the Verb just flips places with the subject to move all the way to the front! For big questions (W-questions), you just stick a question word at the very start.

**CONCEPT**
We are mastering 'W-Fragen' (Who? What? Why?) and 'Ja/Nein-Fragen' (Are you...? Do you...?).

**RULE**
For Ja/Nein questions: Verb goes to Position 1! For W-Fragen: Question word stays in Pos 1, Verb stays in Pos 2.

tables.title: The W-Question Words
tables.note: TIP: All the main ones start with W!

deep_notes.title: WATCH OUT
deep_notes.text: In Yes/No questions, the Verb is the King of the Hill (Position 1). 'Drink you water?' (Trinkst du Wasser?). It feels weird at first because in English we need the word 'Do', but German doesn't need 'Do' at all!

exercises.question: Du fragst nach einer Person: '____ ist das?'
exercises.hint: You are asking about a person (Who).
exercises.explanation: Wer ist das? = Who is that?

exercises.question: Frag 'Are you coming?':
exercises.explanation: Verb in Pos 1 makes it a question!

exercises.question: Du fragst nach dem Befinden: '____ geht es dir?'
exercises.explanation: Wie = How. Use it to ask how someone is doing!

exercises.question: Frag nach einer Sache (What): '____ ist das?'
exercises.explanation: Was ist das? = What is that?
